data.title: Warner Bros. Discovery bid would likely need to be in low to mid $20s/share - report
data.description:
A Paramount Skydance (NASDAQ:PSKY) bid for Warner Bros. Discovery (NASDAQ:WBD) would likely need to be in the low to mid $20s/share to be acceptable to the entertainment giant, according to a CNBC report. 

A lot of the money would have to come from billionaire Oracle co-founder Larry Ellison, CNBC's David Faber said on the business network on Friday. A bid could come as soon as next week. 

Paramount Skydance  (NASDAQ:PSKY) may be trying to make a bid before Warner Bros.' planned split in an attempt to pre-empt a possible bidding war for the studio and streaming unit that could include mega tech companies like Amazon.com (AMZN) and Apple (AAPL), according to the WSJ report on Thursday.

Warner Bros. Discovery CEO David Zaslav wants a bidding war to occur between the parties and met with his bankers at Goldman Sachs earlier this week to gauge interest from the tech behemoths as well as Netflix (NFLX), according to a New York Post report on Friday. Zaslav would like the bids to propel Warner Bros. stock to $40 a share. 

Shares of Warner Bros. Discovery (NASDAQ:WBD) surged almost 30% on news of the possible takeover bid on Thursday and climbed another 15% on Friday.

data.title: 2 out of 4 companies beat EPS estimates this week: Earnings Scorecard
data.description:
The stock market opened mixed on Friday, a day after Wall Street notched up record highs, with rate cut bets firmly in place now.

U.S. stocks set another record closing on Thursday, with the Dow Jones reaching the 46,000 level for the first time, after the consumer inflation report came in higher on a monthly basis in August, while core CPI was steady.

“As we approach the end of the week, markets have been in a buoyant mood over the last 24 hours, continuing into this morning's Asian session, with investor attention squarely focused on the slightly higher than expected U.S. August CPI release and the notably higher than expected jobless claims data,” Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

Earnings Roundup: 

Out of the four S&P 500 companies that reported earnings this week, two of them beat EPS estimates, while three of them surpassed revenue expectations. 

Synopsys (SNPS) reported weaker-than-expected fiscal third-quarter results and guidance.

For the period ending July 31, Synopsys said it earned an adjusted $3.39 per share as revenue rose 14% year-over-year to come in at $1.74B. Analysts had expected the company to earn an adjusted $3.80 per share on $1.77B in revenue.

Looking ahead, Synopsys expects to earn between $2.76 and $2.80 per share on an adjusted basis, while revenue is forecast to be between $2.23B and $2.26B. 

Kroger (KR) said identical-store sales excluding fuel increased 3.4% for the quarter to come in ahead of the expectation of analysts for a rise of 2.8%. Sales growth during the quarter was led by the pharmacy, e-commerce, and fresh food parts of the business. 

EPS was reported at $1.04 vs. $1.00 consensus and $0.93 a year ago.

Oracle (ORCL) reported a massive surge in remaining performance obligations that overshadowed weaker-than-expected fiscal first-quarter results.

For the period ending Aug. 31, the Safra Catz-led company said it earned an adjusted $1.47 per share on $14.93B in revenue. Analysts had expected the company to earn $1.48 per share on $15.03B in revenue.

Overshadowing the results was a massive 359% surge in total remaining performance obligations during the period to $455B. 

Adobe (ADBE) reported its third-quarter fiscal 2025 financial results that surpassed estimates across the board post-market on Thursday. 

For the quarter ended August 29, the design software company reported adjusted earnings per share of $5.31, which was more than the consensus estimate of $5.18. GAAP EPS was $4.18 compared to the $4.05 estimate.

Revenue for the quarter increased 11% year over year to $5.99B versus the consensus of $5.91B.

Earnings next week:  

Several companies are set to report earnings next week, such as Dave & Buster’s Entertainment (NASDAQ:PLAY) and Radiant (NYSE:RLGT) on Monday, Ferguson (NYSE:FERG), Evolution Petroleum (NYSE:EPM), Flux Power Holdings (NASDAQ:FLUX), and Aspen Group (OTCQB:ASPU) on Tuesday. 

Wednesday will include earnings reports from General Mills (NYSE:GIS), Steelcase (NYSE:SCS), and Nano Dimension (NASDAQ:NNDM), with FedEx (NYSE:FDX), Lennar (NYSE:LEN), FactSet Research (NYSE:FDS), and US Gold (NASDAQ:USAU) reporting on Thursday.

data.title: Ares Management plans ~€1.5B credit secondaries transaction - report
data.description:
Ares Management (NYSE:ARES), an alternative asset manager with direct lending activities in Europe, is planning about €1.5 billion worth of credit secondaries transaction, insiders told Bloomberg News.

In the so-called credit secondaries transaction, existing stakes in private credit funds are traded among investors, noted the September 12 report.

Ares' fresh continuation vehicle will hold legacy European loans from its flagship direct-lending strategy, people with knowledge of the matter but not authorized to speak publicly reportedly said.

data.title: Fifth Third Bancorp raises dividend by ~8%
data.description:
• Fifth Third Bancorp (NASDAQ:FITB) declared $0.40/share quarterly dividend, 8.1% increase from prior dividend of $0.37.
  • Forward yield 3.52%
  • Payable Oct. 15; for shareholders of record Sept. 30; ex-div Sept. 30.
  • See FITB Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: WisdomTree launches tokenized fund offering access to private credit
data.description:
WisdomTree (NYSE:WT) rolled out a tokenized fund providing crypto-native investors and institutions exposure to traditionally hard-to-access private credit, it said on Friday.

The WisdomTree Private Credit and Alternative Income Digital Fund (token ticker: CRDT; fund ticker: CRDYX) seeks to track the price and yield performance of the Gapstow Private Credit and Alternative Income Index, an equal-weighted gauge tracking the performance of 35 publicly-traded alternative credit vehicles using an objective, rules-based methodology.

The new fund has a T+0 settlement for subscriptions, T+2 settlement for redemptions, and a minimum investment of $25. At launch, the fund will be tokenized on the Ethereum (ETH-USD) and Stellar (XLM-USD) blockchains.

“CRDT unlocks access to one of the most coveted asset classes – alternatives – directly onchain. By expanding the breadth of our tokenized funds, we’re giving crypto native investors the chance to diversify via exposures that were once reserved for institutions, all within the digital ecosystem,” said Will Peck, head of Digital Assets at WisdomTree.

Tokenization occurs when the ownership of an asset, such as real estate, credit or funds, turns into digital tokens recorded on the blockchain. It's gaining popularity as it makes such traditional investments easier to trade.

data.title: Nvidia reportedly lessens focus on its DGX Cloud service business
data.description:
Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) has reduced its focus on attracting new customers to its cloud computing business, DGX Cloud, according to a report by The Information.

Rather, Nvidia plans to use its cloud business more for internal research uses, such as designing new processors or developing artificial intelligence models, the report said, citing people familiar with the issue.

However, in the same report, Nvidia's Alex Black Bjorlin, who runs DGX Cloud, said there has been no strategy change.

"Our internal researchers need significant computing, so do all of our customers," Bjorlin said. "So our strategy is still the same."

DGX Cloud was launched in March 2023 as a way to provide customers access to clusters of Nvidia's high-powered GPUs. It was initially hosted on Oracle (ORCL) Cloud Infrastructure.

"Nvidia DGX Cloud is a unified AI platform on leading clouds that connects every AI workload to optimized, high-performance, NVIDIA AI infrastructure," according to its website. "Built to handle the most demanding AI workloads from training large language models to serverless inference at scale it accelerates AI application development with integrated software, managed services, and expert guidance."

Nvidia shares inched up 0.5% during Friday morning market action.

data.title: Rapport moved to Buy at Goldman Sachs on antiseizure therapy
data.description:
Rapport Therapeutics (NASDAQ:RAPP) traded higher on Friday after Goldman Sachs assigned the neurology drug developer a Buy rating and a 12-month price target of $51 based on mid-stage trial data the company recently disclosed for its antiseizure therapy RAP-219.

Shares of the Boston, Massachusetts-headquartered biopharma more than doubled in value on Monday after the company said a Phase 2a trial for RAP-219 in patients with drug-resistant focal onset seizures reached the main goal.

Goldman analyst Salveen Richter raised his rating for RAPP to Buy from an early-stage biotech designation, arguing that the readout suggests RAP-219’s potential to reach late-stage development, which the company expects to begin in Q3 2026.

Additionally, Richter highlighted the oral tablet's potential to become a pipeline-in-a-product, with its Phase 2a data for bipolar disorder on track for an H1 2027 readout and an update on the beginning of a Phase 2a trial for diabetic peripheral neuropathic pain expected by the end of 2025. Goldman Sachs projects $10.5B of peak global sales for the candidate across the three indicators.

data.title: U.S. stock retreat from record highs, look for direction as Fed decision looms
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages were seesawing around the flat line on Friday, after the S&P 500 ((SP500), the Nasdaq (COMP:IND), and the Dow (DJI) achieved new record closes. 

Now investors await the Federal Reserve's decision on interest rate cuts, with the markets pricing odd of a September 25 basis point rate cut at 92.4% next week, according to the CME FedWatch Tool. 

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last flat in late afternoon trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.1%, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.2%.

“If the Fed doesn't cut rates multiple times, the economy, especially the job market, will get into even more trouble. If the Fed cuts rates multiple times, the risk of inflation rising again painfully is imminent. In short: the Fed is trapped,” said Marc-André Fongern, former Deutsche Bank and Goldman Sachs investment banker.

In addition, the U.S. Consumer Sentiment Index fell to 55.4 in September from 58.2 in August, missing the 54.9 consensus.

Over in the bond market, the 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 3 basis points to 4.06%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 1 basis point to 3.56%.

Among the top gainers on Friday, Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) and Paramount Skydance (PSKY) were +8.2% and +5.4% respectively on the announcement that the latter is preparing a majority cash bid for the entertainment giant.

U.S. stocks set another record closing on Thursday, with the Dow Jones (DJI) reaching the 46,000 level for the first time, after the consumer inflation report came higher on a monthly basis in August, while core CPI was steady.

More on markets:

data.title: Cocrystal Pharma announces up to $13 million registered direct offering
data.description:
• Cocrystal Pharma (NASDAQ:COCP) has entered into definitive agreements for the purchase and sale of 2.76M shares of its common stock at a purchase price of $1.70 per share in a registered direct offering priced at-the-market under Nasdaq rules.
  • In a concurrent private placement, the company will issue unregistered warrants to purchase up to 5.53M shares of common stock at an exercise price of $1.50 per share that will be exercisable upon issuance and will expire twenty-four months from the effective date of the registration statement covering the resale of the shares of common stock issuable upon exercise of the unregistered warrants.
  • The closing of the offering is expected to occur on or about September 15, 2025.
  • The gross proceeds to the company from the offering, before deducting the placement agent’s fees and other offering expenses payable by the company, are expected to be about $4.7M. The potential additional gross proceeds to the Company from the warrants, if fully-exercised on a cash basis, will be about $8.3 million.
  • COCP is +13.89% to $1.64.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Amerant Bank opens first Miami Beach banking center
data.description:
• Amerant Bank (NYSE:AMTB) on Friday said it opened its first-ever banking center in Miami Beach, located at 427 W 41st Street, bringing its Florida branch count to 21.

  • The 2,838-square-foot banking center sits on Arthur Godfrey Road, a key commercial corridor, and will serve residents, business owners, and visitors.

  • The move follows recent expansions in West Palm Beach, Downtown Miami, and Fort Lauderdale, and comes ahead of a second Miami Beach location in Bay Harbor set to open in late 2025.

  • The bank also plans to open new branches in Tampa and St. Petersburg by early 2026, as it continues broad push across South Florida.

  • AMTB is -1.08% to $21.06.
  • Source: Press Release
